name: Business Research Methods
description: Develops skills for conducting rigorous business and social science research — research design, quantitative and qualitative methods, data analysis, literature review, and professional research reporting.

modules.title: Introduction to Research
modules.objectives: Distinguish types of research (basic, applied, exploratory); explain the general research process; compare business and academic research; identify key ethical principles.
modules.readingNote: Khan Academy — What Is Research? Overview
modules.videoNote: Crash Course Statistics #1 — What Is Statistics?
modules.practiceNote: Google Scholar — Browse and explore academic articles on a topic of your choice
modules.assignment: In 200 words, describe a real business problem and explain whether you would use basic, applied, or exploratory research to investigate it, and why.

modules.title: Formulating Research Questions
modules.objectives: Identify a research problem from a business context; write clear research objectives and hypotheses; define the scope and limitations of a study.
modules.readingNote: Scribbr — How to Write a Research Question
modules.video2Note: University of Melbourne — Developing Research Questions
modules.practiceNote: Google Scholar — Search for examples of business research questions in published studies
modules.assignment: Write one clear research question for a business topic of your choice. Include one hypothesis (null and alternative), and state two scope limitations.

modules.title: Literature Review
modules.objectives: Search academic databases effectively; evaluate source credibility; synthesize findings across multiple sources; manage citations using standard formats.
modules.readingNote: Scribbr — How to Write a Literature Review
modules.video2Note: McMaster Libraries — How to Do a Literature Search
modules.practiceNote: Google Scholar — Practice searching with Boolean operators and filters
modules.assignment: Find 3 peer-reviewed articles on a business topic using Google Scholar. Write a 250-word synthesis paragraph that identifies one shared finding and one point of disagreement.

modules.title: Research Design
modules.objectives: Compare exploratory, descriptive, and causal research designs; explain internal and external validity; distinguish reliability from validity in measurement.
modules.readingNote: Scribbr — Research Design Types and Methods
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Statistics #32 — Validity and Reliability
modules.practiceNote: Google Scholar — Read a published methods section to identify its research design
modules.assignment: Choose a business problem. Write a one-page research design plan specifying the design type (exploratory/descriptive/causal), why it fits, and how you would ensure validity and reliability.

modules.title: Quantitative Methods: Surveys
modules.objectives: Design clear and unbiased survey questions; apply Likert scales correctly; choose an appropriate sampling method; plan survey administration and response rate improvement.
modules.readingNote: Scribbr — Survey Research: Definition, Methods & Examples
modules.video2Note: Scribbr — How to Design a Questionnaire
modules.assignment: Design a 10-question survey on a business topic. Include at least 3 Likert-scale questions. Identify your sampling strategy and explain how you would minimize response bias.

modules.title: Quantitative Methods: Data Collection & Analysis
modules.titleZh: 定量方法：数据收集与分析
modules.objectives: Organize and clean collected data in a spreadsheet; calculate descriptive statistics (mean, median, mode, range, standard deviation); interpret basic inferential results (t-test, correlation).
modules.videoNote: Crash Course Statistics #3 — Measures of Central Tendency
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Statistics #19 — Correlation Doesn't Equal Causation
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Mean, Median, and Mode Practice
modules.assignment: Collect or use a provided dataset of 20+ values. Calculate mean, median, mode, and standard deviation. Write a 200-word paragraph interpreting what these statistics reveal about the data.

modules.title: Qualitative Methods
modules.objectives: Explain when qualitative methods are appropriate; compare interviews, focus groups, observation, and case studies; describe coding and thematic analysis of qualitative data.
modules.readingNote: Scribbr — Qualitative Research: Definition, Methods & Examples
modules.video2Note: University of Auckland — Introduction to Qualitative Methods
modules.practiceNote: Google Scholar — Find and skim a qualitative case study to examine its methods section
modules.assignment: Conduct a 10-minute semi-structured interview with a peer on their study habits or work experience. Write a 200-word summary identifying 2–3 themes from their responses.

modules.title: Mixed Methods Research
modules.objectives: Explain rationale for combining quantitative and qualitative approaches; describe sequential, concurrent, and transformative mixed-methods designs; define triangulation and its role in improving credibility.
modules.readingNote: Scribbr — Mixed Methods Research: Definition, Types & Examples
modules.video2Note: Dr. Bogdan Murswieck — What Is Triangulation in Research?
modules.practiceNote: Google Scholar — Search for a mixed-methods business study and identify how triangulation is used
modules.assignment: Design a brief mixed-methods research plan for a business problem of your choice. Specify your quantitative instrument, qualitative method, and how you would triangulate results.

modules.title: Data Visualization
modules.objectives: Select the correct chart type for a given data set; apply principles of good visualization (clarity, accuracy, avoid chartjunk); create a basic dashboard summarizing research findings.
modules.readingNote: Khan Academy — Displaying and Describing Data
modules.video2Note: Google — Data Visualization with Google Sheets
modules.assignment: Using real or simulated data, create 3 different chart types (e.g., bar, line, pie) for the same dataset. Write a paragraph explaining which visualization is most appropriate and why.

modules.title: Market Research
modules.objectives: Define market research and its role in business decisions; conduct a basic SWOT and PESTLE analysis; analyze consumer behavior patterns; evaluate competitors using secondary data.
modules.readingNote: Khan Academy — What Is Market Research?
modules.videoNote: Crash Course Business: Entrepreneurship #5 — Market Research
modules.practiceNote: Google Scholar — Review published SWOT or PESTLE analyses for real companies
modules.assignment: Select a real company. Complete a full SWOT analysis (4 quadrants, 3 points each) and a PESTLE summary table. Write 150 words on the biggest strategic implication.

modules.title: Financial Research & Analysis
modules.objectives: Read and interpret income statements, balance sheets, and cash flow statements; calculate and interpret key financial ratios; benchmark a company's performance against industry peers.
modules.readingNote: Khan Academy — Accounting and Financial Statements
modules.videoNote: Crash Course Business: Finance #7 — Financial Statements
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Introduction to the Income Statement
modules.assignment: Download a real company's annual report. Calculate 4 financial ratios (e.g., current ratio, profit margin, ROE, debt-to-equity). Write a 200-word analysis of what these ratios reveal.

modules.title: Writing the Research Report
modules.objectives: Structure a business research report (introduction, methodology, findings, discussion, conclusion); apply academic writing style; write objectively, clearly, and concisely; integrate evidence from sources.
modules.videoNote: Scribbr — How to Write a Research Paper: 5 Steps
modules.video2Note: Scribbr — How to Write a Discussion Section
modules.practiceNote: Google Scholar — Find a published business research article and map its structure
modules.assignment: Write a 400-word draft methodology and findings section for your capstone research project. Include how data was collected, how it was analyzed, and 2–3 key findings with supporting evidence.

modules.title: Presenting Research
modules.objectives: Write a professional executive summary; design a clear and compelling slide deck; apply data storytelling principles to communicate findings; handle audience questions confidently.
modules.readingNote: Scribbr — How to Write an Executive Summary
modules.videoNote: Harvard Business Review — The Art of Data Storytelling
modules.video2Note: TEDx — How to Give a Great Research Presentation
modules.practiceNote: Khan Academy — Communication & Presentation Skills for Business
modules.assignment: Create a 6-slide presentation summarizing a research topic. Include: title, research question, methods, 2 findings slides, and conclusion. Write a 100-word executive summary.

modules.title: Capstone: Business Research Project
modules.titleZh: 综合项目：商业研究项目
modules.objectives: Complete a full original business research project from question to presentation; integrate all methods learned; demonstrate proficiency in report writing, data analysis, and public presentation.
modules.readingNote: Google Scholar — Browse capstone business research examples for reference
modules.videoNote: MIT OpenCourseWare — Research Methods in Social Science (overview lecture)
modules.video2Note: Crash Course Statistics #48 — The Research Process (wrap-up)
modules.practiceNote: Google Scholar — Read a full undergraduate-level business research paper as a model
modules.assignment: Submit your complete capstone: (1) a 1500–2000 word research report with all standard sections, (2) a dataset or interview transcripts as an appendix, and (3) a 10-minute oral presentation with slides.

questions.question: Which of the following best describes applied research?
options: Research conducted purely to expand knowledge with no immediate use
Research aimed at solving a specific practical problem
Research that uses only qualitative methods
questions.answer: Research aimed at solving a specific practical problem

questions.question: Which is the correct sequence of the research process?
options: Collect data → Define problem → Analyze → Report
Define problem → Literature review → Design → Collect → Analyze → Report
questions.answer: Define problem → Literature review → Design → Collect → Analyze → Report

questions.question: A research hypothesis is best described as:
options: A proven fact supported by all studies
A testable prediction about the relationship between variables
A research question reworded as a statement
questions.answer: A testable prediction about the relationship between variables

questions.question: Which Boolean operator narrows a Google Scholar search by requiring both terms to appear?
questions.answer: AND

questions.question: Internal validity in research design refers to:
options: How well results apply to other settings
Whether the study actually measures what it claims to measure
The extent to which the sample represents the population
The consistency of results across repeated measurements
questions.answer: Whether the study actually measures what it claims to measure

questions.question: A Likert scale question typically asks respondents to:
options: Choose one answer from a yes/no option
Rank items from most to least important
Rate their level of agreement on a scale (e.g., 1–5)
questions.answer: Rate their level of agreement on a scale (e.g., 1–5)

questions.question: Random sampling is preferable to convenience sampling because it:
options: Ensures every member of the population has an equal chance of being selected
questions.answer: Ensures every member of the population has an equal chance of being selected

questions.question: The mean of the data set {4, 8, 6, 10, 2} is:
Standard deviation measures:
options: The middle value in a dataset
The average distance of data points from the mean
The difference between the maximum and minimum values
questions.answer: The average distance of data points from the mean

questions.question: Which qualitative method involves observing participants in their natural environment without intervening?
questions.answer: Ethnographic observation

questions.question: Focus groups are most useful for:
options: Generating rich qualitative insights through group discussion
questions.answer: Generating rich qualitative insights through group discussion

questions.question: Thematic analysis in qualitative research involves:
options: Running statistical tests on interview data
Calculating the frequency of words in a document
Identifying recurring patterns or themes across qualitative data
questions.answer: Identifying recurring patterns or themes across qualitative data

questions.question: Explain the difference between a research question and a hypothesis. Give one example of each for a business research topic.
Describe two advantages and one disadvantage of using surveys as a data collection method in business research.
What is source credibility and why does it matter in a literature review? Name two criteria for evaluating whether a source is credible.
Which research design is most appropriate when a researcher wants to establish cause and effect?
questions.answer: Causal (experimental)

questions.question: Triangulation in mixed methods research means:
options: Cross-verifying findings using multiple data sources or methods
Drawing a triangle diagram of results
questions.answer: Cross-verifying findings using multiple data sources or methods

questions.question: Which chart type is best for showing change over time?
questions.answer: Line chart

questions.question: PESTLE analysis examines which set of external factors?
options: People, Economics, Strategy, Trade, Law, Environment
Political, Economic, Social, Technological, Legal, Environmental
Product, Entry, Sales, Trade, Location, Equity
Price, Earnings, Stock, Technology, Labor, Exports
questions.answer: Political, Economic, Social, Technological, Legal, Environmental

questions.question: A SWOT analysis is used to:
options: Map a company's internal strengths and weaknesses alongside external opportunities and threats
questions.answer: Map a company's internal strengths and weaknesses alongside external opportunities and threats

questions.question: The current ratio measures:
options: Net profit as a percentage of revenue
A company's ability to pay short-term obligations using current assets
Total debt relative to total equity
Return on investment over one year
questions.answer: A company's ability to pay short-term obligations using current assets

questions.question: Which financial statement shows revenues and expenses over a period?
questions.answer: Income statement

questions.question: In a research report, the methodology section should explain:
options: The implications for future research only
How data was collected, from whom, and how it was analyzed
A summary of all prior studies
The financial cost of the research
questions.answer: How data was collected, from whom, and how it was analyzed

questions.question: An executive summary should:
options: Contain every detail from the full report
Be written before the research is conducted
Concisely summarize the research purpose, methods, key findings, and recommendations
questions.answer: Concisely summarize the research purpose, methods, key findings, and recommendations

questions.question: Data storytelling in a presentation means:
options: Making up data to fit a narrative
Using fictional examples to explain statistics
Framing data insights within a compelling narrative to guide the audience's understanding
Avoiding charts in favor of written text
questions.answer: Framing data insights within a compelling narrative to guide the audience's understanding

questions.question: Which sampling method divides the population into subgroups and samples from each proportionally?
questions.answer: Stratified sampling

questions.question: External validity refers to:
options: Whether the study measures what it intends to measure
The degree to which research findings can be generalized to other contexts or populations
questions.answer: The degree to which research findings can be generalized to other contexts or populations

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of primary data collection?
options: Analyzing a company's historical sales records
questions.answer: Conducting original interviews with consumers

questions.question: Response bias in surveys occurs when:
options: Respondents answer questions inaccurately due to social desirability or misunderstanding
The researcher uses too many Likert questions
Data is analyzed using the wrong statistical test
questions.answer: Respondents answer questions inaccurately due to social desirability or misunderstanding

questions.question: Debt-to-equity ratio is calculated as:
options: Net income divided by total revenue
Total liabilities divided by shareholders' equity
Current assets divided by current liabilities
Operating income divided by interest expense
questions.answer: Total liabilities divided by shareholders' equity

questions.question: Qualitative research is especially valuable when:
options: Precise numerical measurement is the goal
The researcher seeks to understand meanings, experiences, or social processes in depth
Variables can be easily controlled in a lab
questions.answer: The researcher seeks to understand meanings, experiences, or social processes in depth

questions.question: Which principle of good data visualization states that chart elements should not distort the data's true proportions?
questions.answer: Accuracy

questions.question: Describe the structure of a complete business research report and explain the purpose of each major section.
Compare and contrast quantitative and qualitative research methods. Give one situation where each would be more appropriate for a business problem.
Explain the role of market research in business decision-making. How can SWOT and PESTLE analyses complement each other?

quizQuestions.question: Which type of research is conducted primarily to expand general knowledge with no immediate application?
quizQuestions.answer: Basic (pure) research

quizQuestions.question: Business research differs from purely academic research in that it primarily aims to:
options: Solve specific organizational problems or support decisions
quizQuestions.answer: Solve specific organizational problems or support decisions

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following is an ethical principle in research?
options: Fabricating data to support a hypothesis
quizQuestions.answer: Obtaining informed consent from participants

quizQuestions.question: Exploratory research is best suited for:
options: Understanding a problem that is not yet well defined
quizQuestions.answer: Understanding a problem that is not yet well defined

quizQuestions.question: Which step comes immediately after identifying a research problem?
quizQuestions.answer: Reviewing existing literature

quizQuestions.question: A good research question should be:
options: Broad enough to cover every possible topic
Specific, focused, and answerable through research
quizQuestions.answer: Specific, focused, and answerable through research

quizQuestions.question: A null hypothesis typically states:
options: That the researcher's prediction is correct
That there is no significant relationship or difference between variables
The research question in question form
quizQuestions.answer: That there is no significant relationship or difference between variables

quizQuestions.question: Research scope refers to:
options: The total cost of a study
The boundaries that define what the research will and will not cover
The number of hypotheses in a study
The length of the final report
quizQuestions.answer: The boundaries that define what the research will and will not cover

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following is the weakest research question?
options: How does social media advertising affect purchase intention among college students?
What factors influence employee turnover in the retail industry?
How do interest rate changes affect small business loan uptake?
quizQuestions.answer: Is marketing good?

quizQuestions.question: Research objectives are best described as:
options: The final conclusions of the study
Specific statements of what the research aims to achieve
quizQuestions.answer: Specific statements of what the research aims to achieve

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following is the most credible source for a business literature review?
quizQuestions.answer: A peer-reviewed journal article

quizQuestions.question: Google Scholar is primarily used to:
options: Find peer-reviewed academic articles and citations
quizQuestions.answer: Find peer-reviewed academic articles and citations

quizQuestions.question: Synthesizing sources in a literature review means:
options: Identifying connections, contradictions, and gaps across multiple sources
Only summarizing the most recent article
quizQuestions.answer: Identifying connections, contradictions, and gaps across multiple sources

quizQuestions.question: APA citation style is commonly used in:
quizQuestions.answer: Social sciences and business research

quizQuestions.question: Plagiarism in research is best avoided by:
options: Properly citing all sources and quoting directly when necessary
quizQuestions.answer: Properly citing all sources and quoting directly when necessary

quizQuestions.question: A descriptive research design aims to:
options: Accurately describe characteristics of a population or phenomenon
quizQuestions.answer: Accurately describe characteristics of a population or phenomenon

quizQuestions.question: Reliability in research measurement means:
options: The study measures what it claims to measure
Results are consistent when the measurement is repeated under the same conditions
The findings can be generalized globally
quizQuestions.answer: Results are consistent when the measurement is repeated under the same conditions

quizQuestions.question: A causal research design requires:
options: A control group and manipulation of the independent variable
quizQuestions.answer: A control group and manipulation of the independent variable

quizQuestions.question: External validity is threatened when:
options: The researcher uses a large sample
Findings cannot be generalized beyond the specific study context
quizQuestions.answer: Findings cannot be generalized beyond the specific study context

quizQuestions.question: Which design would you use to explore why customers stop using a subscription service when little prior research exists?
quizQuestions.answer: Exploratory

quizQuestions.question: Which question type is most prone to leading the respondent toward a particular answer?
quizQuestions.answer: Leading question

quizQuestions.question: A 5-point Likert scale typically ranges from:
quizQuestions.answer: Strongly agree to strongly disagree

quizQuestions.question: Stratified sampling divides the population into subgroups (strata) to:
options: Ensure proportional representation of key groups
Eliminate the need for random selection
quizQuestions.answer: Ensure proportional representation of key groups

quizQuestions.question: Pilot testing a survey before full deployment helps to:
options: Reduce the number of questions needed
quizQuestions.answer: Identify confusing or ambiguous questions

quizQuestions.question: Response rate in a survey is defined as:
options: The number of questions answered correctly
The percentage of contacted participants who complete the survey
The average time taken to complete the survey
The number of returned surveys minus incomplete ones
quizQuestions.answer: The percentage of contacted participants who complete the survey

quizQuestions.question: The median of the dataset {3, 7, 7, 9, 12} is:
Which measure of central tendency is most affected by extreme outliers?
quizQuestions.answer: Mean

quizQuestions.question: Descriptive statistics are used to:
options: Make predictions about populations from samples
Summarize and describe the features of a dataset
quizQuestions.answer: Summarize and describe the features of a dataset

quizQuestions.question: A positive correlation between two variables means:
options: As one variable increases, the other decreases
As one variable increases, the other tends to increase as well
One variable causes the other to change
quizQuestions.answer: As one variable increases, the other tends to increase as well

quizQuestions.question: Before analyzing data, researchers should 'clean' the dataset by:
options: Checking for missing values, duplicates, and data entry errors
Rounding all numbers to whole values
quizQuestions.answer: Checking for missing values, duplicates, and data entry errors

quizQuestions.question: Which qualitative method is best for understanding individual lived experiences in depth?
quizQuestions.answer: In-depth interview

quizQuestions.question: Case study research is most appropriate when:
options: You need to generalize to a broad population quickly
You want to investigate a real-world phenomenon in its natural context
Statistical testing is the primary goal
quizQuestions.answer: You want to investigate a real-world phenomenon in its natural context

quizQuestions.question: A focus group typically consists of:
quizQuestions.answer: 6–10 participants guided by a moderator

quizQuestions.question: Which qualitative method involves the researcher immersing themselves in a community to observe behavior over time?
quizQuestions.answer: Ethnography

quizQuestions.question: Saturation in qualitative research occurs when:
options: New interviews or observations stop producing new themes or insights
quizQuestions.answer: New interviews or observations stop producing new themes or insights

quizQuestions.question: Mixed methods research combines:
options: Quantitative and qualitative approaches in a single study
quizQuestions.answer: Quantitative and qualitative approaches in a single study

quizQuestions.question: In a sequential mixed methods design, the researcher:
options: Conducts one method first and uses its results to inform the second method
Uses only quantitative data with a qualitative label
Presents qualitative and quantitative results separately without integration
quizQuestions.answer: Conducts one method first and uses its results to inform the second method

quizQuestions.question: Triangulation improves research quality by:
options: Confirming findings through multiple independent data sources or methods
Running the same survey three times
quizQuestions.answer: Confirming findings through multiple independent data sources or methods

quizQuestions.question: Which is a key advantage of mixed methods research?
options: It is always faster than single-method studies
It provides both numerical and in-depth contextual understanding
It eliminates the need for sampling
It removes the need for a literature review
quizQuestions.answer: It provides both numerical and in-depth contextual understanding

quizQuestions.question: When qualitative findings help explain surprising quantitative results, the integration approach is called:
quizQuestions.answer: Explanatory sequential design

quizQuestions.question: A pie chart is most appropriate for showing:
options: Proportions of a whole that add up to 100%
quizQuestions.answer: Proportions of a whole that add up to 100%

quizQuestions.question: Which chart is best for comparing quantities across several distinct categories?
quizQuestions.answer: Bar chart

quizQuestions.question: A scatter plot is used to visualize:
options: Change over time in a single variable
The relationship between two quantitative variables
quizQuestions.answer: The relationship between two quantitative variables

quizQuestions.question: 'Chartjunk' refers to:
options: Too few labels on a graph
Unnecessary visual elements that clutter a chart and obscure data
Using too many colors in a pie chart
Missing a title on a visualization
quizQuestions.answer: Unnecessary visual elements that clutter a chart and obscure data

quizQuestions.question: A histogram differs from a bar chart in that it:
options: Displays the distribution of a continuous variable with no gaps between bars
quizQuestions.answer: Displays the distribution of a continuous variable with no gaps between bars

quizQuestions.question: Primary market research involves:
options: Collecting new data directly from target customers or stakeholders
quizQuestions.answer: Collecting new data directly from target customers or stakeholders

quizQuestions.question: SWOT stands for:
quizQuestions.answer: Strengths, Weaknesses, Opportunities, Threats

quizQuestions.question: Which PESTLE factor would include analyzing minimum wage laws affecting a business?
quizQuestions.answer: Legal

quizQuestions.question: Consumer behavior research examines:
options: The processes by which people select, purchase, and use products
quizQuestions.answer: The processes by which people select, purchase, and use products

quizQuestions.question: Competitive analysis helps a business by:
options: Identifying rivals' strengths and weaknesses to find strategic advantages
quizQuestions.answer: Identifying rivals' strengths and weaknesses to find strategic advantages

quizQuestions.question: Which financial statement shows a company's assets, liabilities, and equity at a specific point in time?
quizQuestions.answer: Balance sheet

quizQuestions.question: Gross profit margin is calculated as:
options: Net income divided by total assets
(Revenue minus cost of goods sold) divided by revenue
Operating income divided by interest expense
Total liabilities divided by shareholders' equity
quizQuestions.answer: (Revenue minus cost of goods sold) divided by revenue

quizQuestions.question: A high debt-to-equity ratio typically indicates:
options: The company has very little debt
The company relies heavily on borrowing to finance its operations
The company has strong cash flow
quizQuestions.answer: The company relies heavily on borrowing to finance its operations

quizQuestions.question: Industry benchmarking involves:
options: Setting internal goals without external comparison
Comparing a company's financial ratios or performance metrics to industry averages or competitors
Calculating only the company's revenue growth
quizQuestions.answer: Comparing a company's financial ratios or performance metrics to industry averages or competitors

quizQuestions.question: The cash flow statement reports:
options: A company's total assets at year-end
Revenues and expenses over a period
Cash inflows and outflows from operating, investing, and financing activities
quizQuestions.answer: Cash inflows and outflows from operating, investing, and financing activities

quizQuestions.question: The methodology section of a research report should include:
options: Details on data collection methods, sample, and analysis approach
quizQuestions.answer: Details on data collection methods, sample, and analysis approach

quizQuestions.question: The discussion section of a research report should:
options: Simply restate the findings in list form
Interpret what the findings mean and connect them to the literature
Describe the data collection process again
quizQuestions.answer: Interpret what the findings mean and connect them to the literature

quizQuestions.question: Academic writing style in business research is characterized by:
options: Casual tone, personal anecdotes, and emotional language
Objective tone, precise language, and evidence-based claims
Bullet points replacing full sentences throughout
quizQuestions.answer: Objective tone, precise language, and evidence-based claims

quizQuestions.question: The conclusion of a research report should:
options: Introduce new data not discussed earlier
Summarize key findings, state limitations, and suggest future research directions
quizQuestions.answer: Summarize key findings, state limitations, and suggest future research directions

quizQuestions.question: Which section of a research report comes immediately after the introduction?
quizQuestions.answer: Methodology

quizQuestions.question: An executive summary is typically placed:
options: At the end of the report as an appendix
At the very beginning of the report, before the introduction
In the middle of the findings section
quizQuestions.answer: At the very beginning of the report, before the introduction

quizQuestions.question: The '5-second rule' in slide design suggests that:
options: Presentations should last no longer than 5 minutes
Each slide should take no more than 5 minutes to read
A good slide communicates its main point within 5 seconds of viewing
Audiences should wait 5 seconds before asking questions
quizQuestions.answer: A good slide communicates its main point within 5 seconds of viewing

quizQuestions.question: Data storytelling in a presentation involves:
options: Presenting only the most favorable results
Using narrative structure to connect data points and guide audience understanding
Reading every number off a spreadsheet
quizQuestions.answer: Using narrative structure to connect data points and guide audience understanding

quizQuestions.question: When handling an unexpected question during a research presentation, it is best to:
options: Make up an answer to appear confident
Acknowledge the question honestly and offer to follow up with research if needed
Argue that the question is irrelevant
quizQuestions.answer: Acknowledge the question honestly and offer to follow up with research if needed

quizQuestions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of a poorly designed presentation slide?
options: A clear chart with a title
Dense paragraphs of text replacing spoken explanation
quizQuestions.answer: Dense paragraphs of text replacing spoken explanation

quizQuestions.question: The first step in a capstone business research project is:
options: Clearly defining the research question and objectives
quizQuestions.answer: Clearly defining the research question and objectives

quizQuestions.question: Which section of the final research report explains who was studied and how data was gathered?
quizQuestions.answer: Methodology

quizQuestions.question: Peer review of a capstone project is valuable because:
options: It replaces the need for a literature review
External feedback helps identify gaps, unclear writing, and analytical errors
It allows the researcher to skip the methodology section
quizQuestions.answer: External feedback helps identify gaps, unclear writing, and analytical errors

quizQuestions.question: Including an appendix in a research report allows the researcher to:
options: Provide supplementary material (raw data, full transcripts) without cluttering the main report
Add personal opinions excluded from the main text
Avoid citing sources in the body
quizQuestions.answer: Provide supplementary material (raw data, full transcripts) without cluttering the main report

quizQuestions.question: A well-executed capstone business research project demonstrates:
options: The ability to copy and summarize one source
Mastery of the full research cycle from question design to presentation of original findings
The ability to design slides without written analysis
quizQuestions.answer: Mastery of the full research cycle from question design to presentation of original findings
